title: dispatch_peer_deliver: chat_id unvalidated, FK error eaten, phantom event emitted
description: relay.rs dispatch_peer_deliver passes peer-supplied chat_id directly to insert_message_ext with no existence check. When FK enforcement fires (chat_id not in chat table), insert returns Err, which is caught, and the original peer-provided message_id is returned as stored_id. The handler then emits MessageReceived with that ID. Result: a message_id is broadcast that does not exist in the DB — receipt tracking breaks, and a malicious MLS peer can inject events into the daemon event channel with arbitrary message IDs by sending PeerDeliver with a nonexistent chat_id. Fix: check chat existence before insert, or distinguish FK errors from other errors and suppress the event on FK failure.
close_reason: chat_id existence check already in place in dispatch_peer_deliver (confirmed by round 27 agent)
